purpose: Controlled release pipeline for turning repeated browser work into a reviewed specialist model or capability without training directly from raw accepted batches.
hardGenes: The CTO-Agent must not train from raw operational traces directly.
Accepted rows and final dataset release must stay separate artifacts.
The deployment target decides the dataset contract: tool-calling browser specialists require transcript-first rows with tools and target turn indices.
Policy bundle, reviewed capability bundle and evaluation gates must be frozen before promotion.

stages.goal: Freeze objective, scope, guardrails, reviewed capability surface and acceptance thresholds.
Collect and review repeated browser traces or accepted action transcripts without treating them as the final training dataset.
Convert accepted records into the exact runtime contract required by the deployment target.
Train the specialist only from the released dataset artifact and persist lineage.
Compare the specialist against the frozen reviewed capability and decide promote, retry or reject.
Promote only after the specialist or reviewed deterministic worker proves stable.

datasetContract.rowRule: Supervise the next assistant turn, not a bare JSON answer, whenever the deployment target is a browser tool-using specialist.

conversions: raw task outputs into tool-calling transcripts
accepted batch rows into train/val/test dataset release
text-only rows into browser-safe transcript rows when the deployment target is browser automation

trainingRuntimePolicy.browserTraining: not in WebGPU browser runtime for this CTO-Agent path
trainingRuntimePolicy.note: WebGPU browser training is treated as too inefficient for this CTO-Agent production path. The first small-model bootstrap target for repeated browser work is Qwen3.5-0.8B.

experimentDiscipline.championDefinition: the currently released reviewed path or trained specialist artifact
experimentDiscipline.challengerDefinition: a bounded candidate change that must beat or match the champion before promotion

promotionGates: evaluation beats or matches the prior path on the target repeated task
